answer: In Yajnavalkya Smriti chapter 1, the dharma of the householder (gṛhastha) is described in section 5, titled 'The Duties of a Householder' (gṛhasthadharmaprakaraṇam), beginning at verse Yj_1.97. It follows the section on the student celibate (brahmacāriprakaraṇam) and covers duties such as daily performance of Smārta rites in the nuptial fire, Śrauta rites in the Vaitānika fires, morning twilight worship, Vedic study, libations to gods and ancestors, the five great sacrifices (mahāyajñas) — to beings, ancestors, gods, Brahman, and men — Bali offerings, hospitality to guests, and related household observances. This section begins at paragraph p-a80877 in work slug 'unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6', chapter 1.
